title: Ranunculus hallstaensis
taxonomy: Ranunculus hallstaensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is classified within the genus Ranunculus and is native to Sweden. The species name 'hallstaensis' indicates its origin from Hallsta, a location in Sweden. Ranunculus hallstaensis is a type of buttercup that has gained attention due to its unique characteristics and conservation status. The plant is considered to be one of the rarest in the world.

preparations: Ranunculus hallstaensis is known to be toxic and should not be used for herbal preparations due to its potential to cause severe symptoms and death if ingested. side_effects: Ranunculus hallstaensis is highly toxic due to the presence of ranunculin and other glucosinolates. Ingestion of the plant can cause severe oral and gastrointestinal irritation. If it comes into contact with the skin, it can cause painful blistering and burns. The toxins can also cause systemic effects, such as vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, if ingested in large quantities. As the toxins can be absorbed through the skin, handling the plant should be done with caution.
